title: Permits & Registration
summary: What you need before travelling to Kashmir.

sections.heading: For Indian nationals
sections.content: No special permit is required for Srinagar, Gulmarg, Pahalgam, Sonamarg, or other standard tourist circuits. Carry a valid government photo ID (Aadhaar, passport, or driving licence).

sections.heading: For foreign nationals
sections.content: Register at the FRRO (Foreigners Registration Office) in Srinagar within 24 hours of arrival if staying more than 14 days. Some restricted areas near the LoC require separate permits — check with the DC office before planning offbeat routes.

sections.heading: Amarnath Yatra
sections.content: Separate registration mandatory via the SASB portal. Medical certificate required. Yatra typically runs July–August.

title: Getting There
summary: Air, road, and rail connections to the valley.

sections.heading: By air
sections.content: Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport (SXR) in Srinagar connects to Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, and other major cities. Pre-paid taxi counters are outside arrivals — fixed rates to city centre (~₹800–1200).

sections.heading: By road
sections.content: NH-44 from Jammu to Srinagar (approx. 270 km, 8–10 hrs depending on traffic and weather). Jawahar Tunnel and Banihal pass can see delays in winter. Shared sumos run from Jammu bus stand.

sections.heading: By train
sections.content: Nearest major railhead: Jammu Tawi (JAT). From Jammu, take road transport to Srinagar. Udhampur–Baramulla rail link is partially operational — check IRCTC for current status.

sections.heading: Spring (Mar – May)
sections.content: Tulip Garden in full bloom (usually April). Mild weather, fewer crowds than summer. Some higher routes still snow-bound.

sections.heading: Summer (Jun – Aug)
sections.content: Peak season. Book hotels 3–4 weeks ahead. Amarnath Yatra traffic affects Pahalgam. Day temperatures 20–30°C; carry a light jacket for evenings.

sections.heading: Autumn (Sep – Nov)
sections.content: Chinar leaves turn gold — photographers' favourite. Harvest season in orchards. Gulmarg gondola usually still running.

sections.heading: Winter (Dec – Feb)
sections.content: Gulmarg ski season. Srinagar sees occasional snowfall; houseboats have bukhari heating. Check flight cancellations during heavy snow.

title: Local Etiquette
summary: Respectful travel in a culturally rich region.

sections.heading: Dress & behaviour
sections.content: Modest dress appreciated, especially near religious sites. Ask before photographing people — many are happy to oblige but it's courtesy to check.

sections.heading: Bargaining
sections.content: Expected at handicraft shops and shikara rates. Fixed prices at government emporiums (J&K Govt Arts Emporium on Residency Road is reliable for papier-mâché and shawls).

sections.heading: Food notes
sections.content: Wazwan is the traditional feast — usually needs advance order for groups. Local kahwa (saffron tea) is offered everywhere; accepting a cup is a nice gesture. Pure veg options exist but are limited outside Srinagar.

sections.heading: Helplines
sections.content: J&K Tourism Helpline: 1800-180-7777 (toll-free). Tourist Police Srinagar: 0194-245-2000. Emergency: 112 (single emergency number).

sections.heading: Health
sections.content: SKIMS (Soura) and SMHS Hospital handle emergencies in Srinagar. Altitude sickness possible above 3,000 m — acclimatise in Gulmarg before long treks. Pharmacies common in Srinagar; carry basic meds if heading remote.

sections.heading: Connectivity
sections.content: 4G available in Srinagar and major towns. Spotty coverage in Sonamarg upper reaches and some Pahalgam trails. Download offline maps before leaving city limits.

sections.heading: Year-round
sections.content: Photo ID, sunscreen (high altitude), reusable water bottle, cash (ATMs in towns but not everywhere), power bank.

sections.heading: Summer add-ons
sections.content: Light rain jacket, comfortable walking shoes, hat. Umbrella useful for sudden showers.

sections.heading: Winter add-ons
sections.content: Thermal layers, waterproof boots, lip balm, gloves. Gulmarg needs proper winter gear if skiing — rentals available but sizes limited during peak.
